title: Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

We are running podman rootless on a container. When run podman build we get the error below unless we run previously podman login or podman pull. 
Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout: stderr: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:12 for /var/spool/mail): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /var/spool/mail: invalid argument

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

2.

3.

comments.body:
This means that you are pulling an image with more UIDs/GIDs then are available within your user namespace.
From the Podman info it looks like you have enough.  Did you change the /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid file after you ran your first container.  If so, could you try

podman system migrate

And see if that fixes your problem?
@rhatdan what do you mean by "after you ran your first containet". If I run podman login or podman pull before podman build then it works. I did not change /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid

Do you still see this issue, IE If you do a podman logout, does the cause the issue to reappear.  I really see the relationship for your solution to the stated problem. The stated problem usually happens when /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid are configured correctly.

@GolanTrevize10 are you still seeing the issue?

Cc: @giuseppe

it looks to me there are just not enough IDs available to the user.

What is the output for `podman unshare cat /proc/self/?id_map`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
got no feedback, closing the issue
